questions.text:
The size of a file can be measured using different data storage units.

Tick (✓) one box to show the smallest data storage unit.

A byte
B gibibyte (GiB)
C kibibyte (KiB)
D nibble

marking_points.text: D

questions.text:
The size of a file can be measured using different data storage units.

State how many tebibytes (TiB) are equal to 2 pebibytes (PiB).

The size of a file can be measured using different data storage units. The size of a file needs to be reduced.

Give the name of the process that is used to reduce the size of a file.

marking_points.text: (Data) Compression

questions.text:
The size of a file can be measured using different data storage units. The size of a file needs to be reduced. One reason the size of a file may need reducing is so that it has a shorter transmission time.

Give one other reason why the size of a file may need reducing.

marking_points.text: Less bandwidth required to transmit the file
Less storage space needed to store the file
To meet email file size limit
To use less data allowance

questions.text:
A clothes store has a ticket system to allow customers to speak to a personal shopper. The ticket displays a ticket number in hexadecimal, for example 11B. The ticket system has an output device that creates a paper ticket.

Identify an appropriate output device to produce the paper ticket.

marking_points.text: Printer

questions.text:
A clothes store has a ticket system to allow customers to speak to a personal shopper. The ticket displays a ticket number in hexadecimal, for example 11B. Each ticket number is stored as a binary number in the ticket machine. Two ticket numbers are 14B and 97A.

Convert the two ticket numbers from hexadecimal to binary.
14B
97A

A clothes store has a ticket system to allow customers to speak to a personal shopper. The ticket displays a ticket number in hexadecimal, for example 11B. Each ticket number is stored as a binary number in the ticket machine.

Give two reasons why the ticket number is displayed as hexadecimal instead of binary.
1
2

marking_points.text: Easier/quicker for the customer to understand/read/remember
Takes up less space on the ticket // Shorter representation

questions.text:
A clothes store has a ticket system to allow customers to speak to a personal shopper. The ticket displays a ticket number in hexadecimal, for example 11B. The ticket numbers could also be represented as denary.

Convert the two ticket numbers from hexadecimal to denary.
02C
10B

A clothes store has a ticket system to allow customers to speak to a personal shopper. The ticket displays a ticket number in hexadecimal, for example 11B. The ticket numbers could also be represented as denary.

Convert the two denary numbers into hexadecimal.
109
415

An image is converted to binary to be represented by a computer.

Complete the table with the missing terms and definition about representing an image.

Term | Definition
(blank) | This is the smallest component of an image.
resolution | (blank)
(blank) | This is the number of bits used to create each colour in an image.
(blank) | This is additional data that is stored with an image that can provide information such as the time and date the image was taken.

rows.definition: This is the smallest component of an image.
This is the number of bits used to create each colour in an image.
This is additional data that is stored with an image that can provide information such as the time and date the image was taken.

answers.scoring_rule: One mark for each correct term or definition.

marking_points.text: Term: pixel — Definition: It is the smallest component of an image.
Term: resolution — Definition: The number of pixels high by the number of pixels wide // The number of pixels in an image
Term: colour depth // bit depth — Definition: This is the number of bits that are used to create each colour in an image.
Term: metadata // header — Definition: This is additional data that is stored with an image that can provide information such as the time and date the image was taken.

questions.text:
A restaurant has an electronic ordering system. The system allows customers to select the food they want to order. The data for the order is transmitted across their network to a computer in the restaurant kitchen. The data is sent using serial full-duplex data transmission.

Give two reasons why serial full-duplex is a suitable data transmission method for the data.
1
2
questions.topic_name: Types and Methods of Data Transmission

answers.scoring_rule: Two from (one mark for serial, one mark for full-duplex):

marking_points.text: Serial is suitable as data may be travelling a long distance (over 5 metres).
Serial is suitable to make sure that bits don't arrive skewed/arrives in order.
Serial is suitable as there is less chance of error.
Serial has sufficient transmission speed as not much data being sent.
Full-duplex is suitable as data needs to go between customer and kitchen computer // data needs to go in both directions // so notification of errors can be sent back to customer.

questions.text:
A restaurant has an electronic ordering system. The system allows customers to select the food they want to order. The data for the order is transmitted across their network to a computer in the restaurant kitchen. The data is sent using serial full-duplex data transmission.

Give one improvement that would be made to the data transmission if parallel transmission is used instead of serial transmission.
questions.topic_name: Types and Methods of Data Transmission

marking_points.text: The data may be transmitted faster.

questions.text:
A restaurant has an electronic ordering system. A checksum is used to check the data for errors after transmission.

Describe the process of the checksum error detection method.

marking_points.text: The checksum is calculated from/using the data.
… using an algorithm // by example.
The (checksum) value is transmitted with the data.
The (checksum) value is recalculated after transmission/by recipient.
If the checksum values do not match, an error is detected // If the checksum values match, no error is detected …
… request for data to be resent if there is an error.

questions.text:
A restaurant has an electronic ordering system. A checksum is used to check the data for errors after transmission.

Identify one other error detection method that could be used to check for errors in the data.

marking_points.text: Parity byte/block check
Echo check

questions.text:
A restaurant has an electronic ordering system. A customer can also order food from home using the internet. The data for their order is broken down into packets and transmitted across the internet to the computer in the restaurant kitchen.

Tick (✓) one box to show which item would not be included in a packet's header.

A destination address
B originator's address
C packet number
D payload
questions.topic_name: Types and Methods of Data Transmission

marking_points.text: D

questions.text:
A restaurant has an electronic ordering system. A customer can also order food from home using the internet. The data for their order is broken down into packets and transmitted across the internet to the computer in the restaurant kitchen. The packets of data may need to be reordered when they arrive at the computer in the restaurant kitchen.

Explain why the packets of data may need to be reordered.
questions.topic_name: Types and Methods of Data Transmission

marking_points.text: The packets arrive in the wrong order.
Each packet could take a different route.
Some packets may take longer than others to be transmitted.
Some packets may not arrive/be corrupted and need to be requested again.

questions.text:
Data is processed by the central processing unit (CPU) in a computer that has a Von Neumann architecture.

Complete the table with the missing terms and definitions about processing data.

Term | Definition
current instruction register (CIR) | (blank)
(blank) | This is a processing unit within the CPU that can fetch, decode and execute instructions.
(blank) | This is a list of all the commands that can be processed by the CPU.
cache | (blank)
accumulator | (blank)

rows.definition: This is a processing unit within the CPU that can fetch, decode and execute instructions.
This is a list of all the commands that can be processed by the CPU.

marking_points.text: current instruction register (CIR): stores the instruction that is being decoded/executed
Term: core — Definition: This is a processing unit within the CPU that can fetch, decode and execute instructions.
Term: instruction set — Definition: This is a list of all the commands that can be processed by the CPU.
cache: stores frequently used data/instructions
accumulator: stores the result of interim calculations

questions.text:
Data is processed by the central processing unit (CPU) in a computer that has a Von Neumann architecture.

Identify the component in the CPU that is responsible for decoding an instruction.

marking_points.text: Control unit // CU

questions.text:
Data is processed by the central processing unit (CPU) in a computer that has a Von Neumann architecture.

Explain how the clock speed can affect the performance of a CPU.

marking_points.text: On each clock pulse, an FDE cycle is performed.
The clock speed is the number of FDE cycle performed in a second // The clock speed is the number of times the clock pulses per second/per unit of time.
If clock speed is increased more FDE cycles can be performed in a second // If clock speed is decreased fewer FDE cycles can be performed in a second.
If clock speed is increased, it can improve the performance of the CPU // If clock speed is decreases it decreases the performance of the CPU.

questions.text:
Data can be encrypted using asymmetric encryption.

Complete the paragraph about asymmetric encryption. Use the terms from the list. Some of the terms in the list will not be used. You may use a term more than once.

algorithm binary cipher text compression error hardware hexadecimal meaningless plain text private public readable transmission unreadable

Data is encrypted using an encryption key. This is a type of [blank] that scrambles the [blank] [blank] and turns it into [blank] . This makes the data [blank] . A [blank] key is used to encrypt the data. This key cannot be used to decrypt the data. A [blank] key is used to decrypt the data. Any device is able to request the [blank] key, but only your device knows the [blank] key.

answers.scoring_rule: One mark for each correct term in the correct position

marking_points.text: algorithm
plain text
cipher text
meaningless
public/private
private/public (must be opposite of MP5)
public
private

questions.text:
Data can be encrypted using asymmetric encryption. Data can also be encrypted using symmetric encryption.

Give one reason why a person would use asymmetric encryption instead of symmetric encryption.

marking_points.text: The process is more secure.
Do not need to worry about key exchange.

questions.text:
A student's computer has primary storage. One example of primary storage is random access memory (RAM).

Identify one other example of primary storage.

marking_points.text: Read only memory // ROM
Cache

questions.text:
A student's computer has primary storage. One example of primary storage is random access memory (RAM).

Explain why RAM is an example of primary storage and not secondary storage.

marking_points.text: RAM is directly accessed by the CPU.
… whereas secondary storage is not directly accessed by the CPU.
All secondary storage is non-volatile storage.
… whereas RAM is volatile storage.

questions.text:
A student's computer has primary storage. The student is editing a large video file for a project and during this process the RAM becomes full. This makes the computer crash.

Describe how virtual memory could be used in this process to stop the computer crashing.

marking_points.text: Virtual memory can act as an extension to the RAM.
The secondary storage device is partitioned to create the virtual memory.
Pages can be transferred from the RAM to the virtual memory …
… to stop the RAM getting full // … when the RAM is full.

questions.text:
A student's computer has primary storage. The computer uses solid-state (flash memory) storage.

Give two features of solid-state (flash memory) storage.
1
2

marking_points.text: It has no moving parts.
It uses semiconductor chips.
It uses NAND/NOR/Flip-flops technology.
It uses cells and transistors (that are laid out in a grid).
It uses control gates and floating gates.
Stores data as electrical charges.

questions.text:
A student's computer has primary storage. The computer uses solid-state (flash memory) storage.

Give two examples of solid-state (flash memory) storage.
1
2

marking_points.text: Solid-state drive // SSD
USB flash memory drive
SD card

questions.text:
A robot is used to move car parts in a factory. One characteristic of the robot is that it has a mechanical structure.

Give one other characteristic of a robot.

marking_points.text: It has electrical components. // by example
It is programmable.
It can move.

questions.text:
A robot is used to move car parts in a factory. The robot has firmware.

State what is meant by firmware.

marking_points.text: Permanent instructions/software that are programmed into/stored in the ROM.
Instructions/software that allow hardware to be controlled/managed.
Instructions/software that provides the operating system with a platform to run on.

questions.text:
A robot is used to move car parts in a factory. Interrupts are generated when the robot is operating in the factory.

Give two examples of interrupts that may be generated when the robot is in operation.
1
2

marking_points.text: user input // by example
encountering an obstacle
emergency stop
hardware error
software error
battery low
component overheating
two processes accessing the same location

questions.text:
A robot is used to move car parts in a factory.

Give two advantages to the workers in the factory of using robots to move the car parts.
1
2

marking_points.text: Workers don't need to lift heavy machinery
The workers are less likely to injure themselves
The workers don't need to be in any dangerous environments in the factory
The workers don't need to perform boring/repetitive tasks such as moving car parts
Workers can focus on having higher expertise skills
Create new jobs for workers to maintain the robots.

questions.text:
A robot is used to move car parts in a factory. The robots have the ability to automatically adapt their own processes.

State the name of the ability described.

marking_points.text: Machine learning

questions.text:
Each employee in a finance company has a computer and an electronic account. Each computer has an operating system. One function of the operating system is to manage memory.

Describe the role of the operating system in managing memory.

marking_points.text: To make sure memory is used efficiently
Allocates/deallocates memory to processes
Checks that the processes being actioned have enough memory available
Moves data to and from memory/between memory and storage
Makes sure that two processes do not try and access the same memory location
Creates a memory partition
Creates virtual memory
Transmits pages between RAM and VM

questions.text:
Each employee in a finance company has a computer and an electronic account. The finance company is concerned that a hacker may perform a brute-force attack to gain access to an employee's account.

Describe how the hacker would perform the brute-force attack.

marking_points.text: Try to find out/guess a password
… by repeatedly entering different combinations/passwords
Could use software to do it automatically

questions.text:
Each employee in a finance company has a computer and an electronic account. The finance company is concerned that a hacker may perform a brute-force attack to gain access to an employee's account.

Give three cyber security solutions that could be used to help prevent a brute-force attack from being successful.
1
2
3

marking_points.text: Strong passwords // passwords that are long and have a combination of letters, numbers and symbols
Two-step verification // two-factor authentication
Biometric password
Limiting the number of login attempts

questions.text:
Each employee in a finance company has a computer and an electronic account. The company uses a digital currency to purchase a new car.

State what is meant by a digital currency.

marking_points.text: A currency that only exists electronically/virtually

questions.text:
Each employee in a finance company has a computer and an electronic account. The company uses a digital currency to purchase a new car. The digital currency uses a process that involves a digital ledger. The ledger has time stamped transactions that cannot be altered.

Give the name of this process.

marking_points.text: Blockchain
